title: Profiling Metazoan Transcription Genome-Wide with Nucleotide Resolution Using NET-seq
author: Danya J Martell
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bpymmpu6

steps.title: Prepare the DNA control ladder
steps.instruction: Prepare the DNA control ladder. Add 1.0 µl of DNA control ladder to 9 µl of RNase-free H2O. Add 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer and mix.

steps.title: Add 20 µl of 2× TBU...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer to the cDNA sample and the oGAB11 cDNA control, mix well.

steps.title: Denature the cDNA sample, oGAB11 cDNA...
steps.instruction: Denature the cDNA sample, oGAB11 cDNA control and DNA control ladder for 3 min at 95 °C in a Thermomixer. Place the samples on ice.

steps.title: Prerun a 10% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea...
steps.instruction: Prerun a 10% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel at 200 V for 15 min in 1× TBE. Thoroughly flush out gel wells prior to pre-running the gel (see Note 35).

steps.title: Load gel (20 uL per lane,...
steps.instruction: Load gel (20 uL per lane, each sample will be split into two lanes) with the cDNA sample, the oGAB11 cDNA control and the DNA ladder. Separate by PAGE at 200 V for 65 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light.

steps.title: For the cDNA sample, excise region...
steps.instruction: For the cDNA sample, excise region between ~85 and 160 nt (use oGAB11 as baseline and cut up ~70 nt). For the cDNA oGAB11 control, excise a narrow band ~85 nt. If no cDNA smear is observed see Note 37.

steps.title: Perform rapid gel extraction on the...
steps.instruction: Perform rapid gel extraction on the excised gel (see Box 3). There will be two gels for one sample since each sample was split into two lanes, combine the two gels from one sample into a single tube.

steps.title: After rapid gel extraction is performed,...
steps.instruction: After rapid gel extraction is performed, use the Oligo kit (Zymo) and elute cDNA from column with 15 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) (at this point, the cDNA can be stored indefinitely at −20 °C). Alternatively, cDNA can be prepared by precipitation (see Note 33).

steps.title: Prepare circularization mix and store it on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare circularization mix and store it on ice.

steps.title: In a 0
steps.instruction: In a 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tube, add 4 µl of circularization mix to 15 µl of the cDNA sample and the oGAB11 cDNA control sample, mix well.

steps.title: Add 1 µl of CircLigase (100 U/µl) and mix
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of CircLigase (100 U/µl) and mix.

steps.title: Incubate the CircLigase reaction for 60...
steps.instruction: Incubate the CircLigase reaction for 60 min at 60 °C and then 10 min at 80 °C in a thermal cycler. Circularized cDNA can be stored indefinitely at −20 °C.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: at this point a specific depletion of highly abundant mature RNAs can be performed (see Note

steps.title: Perform PCR amplification test (Box 4)
steps.instruction: Perform PCR amplification test (Box 4)

steps.title: Prepare PCR mix for four PCR...
steps.instruction: Prepare PCR mix for four PCR amplification reactions for both the cDNA sample and the oGAB11 control cDNA sample. Mix well and store on ice.

steps.title: For each PCR, put 19 µl...
steps.instruction: For each PCR, put 19 µl of PCR master mix in a 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well.

steps.title: Perform PCR amplifications (as shown in...
steps.instruction: Perform PCR amplifications (as shown in Table 2) with determined optimal number of PCR cycles (see Box 4).

steps.title: Prepare DNA control ladder
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA control ladder. Add 1.0 µl of DNA control ladder to 9 µl of DNase-free H2O. Add 2 µl of 6× DNA loading dye and mix well.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.4 µl of 6× DNA loading dye to each PCR sample tube and mix well.

steps.title: Load gel with the PCR products and DNA control ladder
steps.instruction: Load gel with the PCR products and DNA control ladder. Separate by TBE gel electrophoresis on an 8% (wt/vol) TBE gel at 180 V for 55 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light.

steps.title: For each sample, excise the band...
steps.instruction: For each sample, excise the band from the gel that contains the PCR product. The NET-seq library is a broad band that runs at ~150 nt (use oGAB as baseline and cut up ~50 nt). Avoid contamination from the lower band that runs at ~120 nt, representing PCR product from empty circles (see Note 39). The oGAB11 control sample is not subjected to this step, or any further steps.

steps.title: Perform the first four steps of...
steps.instruction: Perform the first four steps of rapid gel extraction (see Box 3)

steps.title: After centrifugation, add 670 µl of...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, add 670 µl of DNA soaking buffer and mix.

steps.title: Incubate the samples overnight at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples overnight at room temperature in a Thermomixer (set at 1,500 r.p.m.).

steps.title: Use a cut 1,000 µl pipette...
steps.instruction: Use a cut 1,000 µl pipette tip to transfer the gel slurry into a microcentrifuge tube filter.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 3 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 3 min at room temperature. Transfer the eluate to a new 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Precipitate the NET-seq library by adding...
steps.instruction: Precipitate the NET-seq library by adding 2 µl of 15 mg/ml GlycoBlue and 680 µl of isopropanol. Mix well then incubate the precipitations at −20 °C for ≥1 h (the DNA precipitations can be stored at −20 °C overnight).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 30 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 30 min at 4 °C to pellet the RNA.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and wash the pellet with 750 µl of 80% (vol/vol) ice-cold ethanol.

steps.title: Spin the sample at 20,000g for...
steps.instruction: Spin the sample at 20,000g for 2 min at 4 °C, and then remove the supernatant. Air-dry the RNA pellet for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Resuspend the NET-seq library in 7...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the NET-seq library in 7 - 10 µl of Tris-HCl (10 mM, pH 8.0). The DNA sequencing library can be stored indefinitely at −20 °C.

steps.title: Prepare a 1:5 dilution of the...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1:5 dilution of the NET-seq library by adding 1 µl of the NET-seq library to 4 µl of Tris-HCl (10 mM, pH 8.0); mix well (see Note 40).

steps.title: Use 1 µl of the diluted...
steps.instruction: Use 1 µl of the diluted NET-seq library for quantification with the Qubit fluorometer using the Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit. Prepare the sample and perform the measurement according to the manufacturer’s protocol (see Note 41).

steps.title: Use 1 µl of the diluted...
steps.instruction: Use 1 µl of the diluted NET-seq library for characterization on the Agilent Bioanalyzer; use the high-sensitivity DNA analysis kit according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Sequence using oLSC006
steps.instruction: Sequence using oLSC006. Sequence the human NET-seq library from the 3′ end (single-end sequencing) on the Illumina platform using oLSC006 (Table 1) as a custom sequencing primer. Perform sequencing according to the manufacturer’s instructions. NET-seq libraries are typically sequenced on MiSeq, HiSeq or NextSeq next-generation sequencing platforms. A reasonable coverage is obtained with 100–200 million reads.

steps.title: Below we describe in brief the...
steps.instruction: Below we describe in brief the custom bioinformatics pipeline (see Figure 5) that is used to generate NET-seq coverage files. The scripts are available at http://github.com/churchmanlab/MiMB2019NETseq . See Note 42 for a description of the improvements relative to our previous pipeline (Mayer et al. 2015) .

steps.title: Assess read quality control with FastQC
steps.instruction: Assess read quality control with FastQC.

steps.title: Displace 5’ end random barcode sequence...
steps.instruction: Displace 5’ end random barcode sequence from sequence to read identifier line and save as a separate fastq file. This barcode serves as a unique molecular identifier (UMI) and originates from the linker (see Table 1).

steps.title: Align fastq files with and without...
steps.instruction: Align fastq files with and without UMI sequences separately to a human reference genome using STAR (See Note 43). In case a Drosophila spike-in was used, concatenate the human and Drosophila fasta files to generate a combined reference genome.

steps.title: Remove alignments resulting from RT mispriming events, i
steps.instruction: Remove alignments resulting from RT mispriming events, i.e. cases where RT priming did not occur at the complementary sequence site on the ligated linker but instead at a position within the RNA fragment. The resulting reads can be identified as they lack the random UMI sequence encoded on the linker and therefore align well to the reference genome both with and without UMI displacement: when the STAR alignment score of a read with the UMI sequence equals the UMI length (10nt) plus the alignment score from that read without UMI.

steps.title: Remove alignments that map to multiple...
steps.instruction: Remove alignments that map to multiple genomic locations by filtering for their MAPQ value (< 50) using samtools.

steps.title: Remove alignments resulting from PCR duplication events, i
steps.instruction: Remove alignments resulting from PCR duplication events, i.e. cases of multiple amplicons that originate from the same RNA fragment. The resulting multiple reads are bioinformatically indistinguishable as their alignments share the same position, strand, chromosome, UMI sequence and CIGAR score. To filter out these PCR duplicates, we maintain only one of the alignments.

steps.title: Remove alignments that could arise from splicing intermediates, i
steps.instruction: Remove alignments that could arise from splicing intermediates, i.e. 3’ ends of annotated exons and introns.

steps.title: Use HTseq to generate a coverage...
steps.instruction: Use HTseq to generate a coverage (bedgraph) file containing the read counts at a base-pair resolution over the reference genome as determined from the 5’ end of the read alignments (without UMI) on the opposite strand. The resulting positions correspond to the 3’ ends of nascent RNA and thereby map RNA polymerase positions. The coverage files will be used for a variety of downstream bioinformatics analyses such as visual inspection (e.g. using IGV), metagene profiling (e.g. Bedtools), differential expression (e.g. DEseq2).

steps.title: The western blot control is used...
steps.instruction: The western blot control is used to determine whether or not the cellular fractionation protocol has been executed successfully. The efficiency of the fractionation can be quantified by measuring the amount of elongating Pol II captured in the chromatin fraction (ideally≥95%). When the fractionation protocol is being performed for the first time or being tested on a new cell type, the cytoplasm, nucleoplasm, and chromatin fractions should be checked for subcellular marker proteins (see Note 5) to confirm the fractionation is working (see Note 20).

steps.title: and chromatin fraction (from 3
steps.instruction: and chromatin fraction (from 3.3.

steps.title: volumes to the cytoplasmic fraction (from 3
steps.instruction: volumes to the cytoplasmic fraction (from 3.3.

steps.title: volume by adding 1× PBS
steps.instruction: volume by adding 1× PBS. Mix each fraction well and ensure the chromatin pellet is completely resuspended (see Note 7).

steps.title: Add 50 µl of 2× SDS buffer (see section 2
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl of 2× SDS buffer (see section 2.3.2 step 7) to 50 µl of each subcellular fraction and boil the samples at 95 °C for 5 min (at this point, samples can be stored for months at −20 °C).

steps.title: Separate samples by standard SDS-PAGE (loading...
steps.instruction: Separate samples by standard SDS-PAGE (loading 10 µl of each boiled sample per lane is usually sufficient).

steps.title: Probe the membrane overnight at 4...
steps.instruction: Probe the membrane overnight at 4 °C with Pol II CTD Ser2-P antibody (3E10, 1:1,000 dilution) and Pol II CTD Ser5-P antibody (3E8, 1:1,000 dilution). These antibodies target transcribing RNA Pol II (see Note 6).

steps.title: Performing RNA fragmentation results in a...
steps.instruction: Performing RNA fragmentation results in a homogeneous RNA pool which helps to avoid length biases in any downstream enzymatic reactions. The fragmentation test can be performed using total RNA (or any easily-obtained RNA sample). Once the appropriate fragmentation time is determined (where most of the RNA molecules range from 40 –110 nt), fragmentation can be performed on the nascent RNA (obtained from cellular fractionation) using the determined time (Figure 2); the size range of the nascent RNA and total RNA pools do not vary enough for the fragmentation time to be different.

steps.title: Take 9 samples of RNA from...
steps.instruction: Take 9 samples of RNA from a total RNA sample (or any other easily obtained RNA sample), each sample should be 20 µl in volume and contain ~1ug of RNA. Add 20 µl of 2× alkaline fragmentation solution to each sample and mix well (see Note 32); fragmentation is not performed for the oGAB11 control.

steps.title: Fragment each RNA sample at 95...
steps.instruction: Fragment each RNA sample at 95 °C in a thermal cycler for the following times: 0, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, and 50 minutes.

steps.title: To purify the fragmented RNA, use...
steps.instruction: To purify the fragmented RNA, use the RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit (Zymo) and elute RNA from column with 10 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.0). Alternatively, RNA can be prepared by RNA precipitation (see Note 33).

steps.title: Prepare RNA ladder (denoted as L...
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA ladder (denoted as L in figure) by adding 1.0 µl of RNA control ladder to 9 µl of RNase-free H2O. Add 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample to the RNA ladder and mix well.

steps.title: Prepare all RNA samples by adding 1
steps.instruction: Prepare all RNA samples by adding 1.0 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer to each sample, mix well.

steps.title: Denature the RNA sample and RNA...
steps.instruction: Denature the RNA sample and RNA ladder for 2 min at 80 °C. Place the samples on ice.

steps.title: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea...
steps.instruction: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel at 200 V for 15 min in 1× TBE. Thoroughly flush out gel wells prior to pre-running the gel (see Note 35).

steps.title: Load gel with the RNA ladder...
steps.instruction: Load gel with the RNA ladder and the fragmented RNA sample. Separate by PAGE at 200 V for 65 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light. The optimal fragmentation time is when most RNA molecules are in the required size range, between 40 and 110 nt (the optimal fragmentation time in the figure below is between 25 and 30 minutes).

steps.title: This protocol for rapidly extracting RNA...
steps.instruction: This protocol for rapidly extracting RNA or cDNA from a polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel is similar to the gel extraction protocol described by the Weissman laboratory (Ingolia et al. 2012; Churchman and Weissman 2012) .

steps.title: Use a 21G needle to pierce...
steps.instruction: Use a 21G needle to pierce the bottom of a 0.5-ml RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Place the pierced 0
steps.instruction: Place the pierced 0.5-ml tube into a 1.5-ml RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Put the gel slice (from fragmentation,...
steps.instruction: Put the gel slice (from fragmentation, RT, or PCR) into the pierced 0.5-ml tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the nestled tubes, containing the...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the nestled tubes, containing the gel slice, at 20,000g for 4 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 200 µl of RNase-free H2O...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl of RNase-free H2O to the chopped-up gel and mix.

steps.title: Incubate the sample mixture for 10...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample mixture for 10 min at 70 °C in a Thermomixer.

steps.title: Vortex the mixture for 30 s at medium intensity
steps.instruction: Vortex the mixture for 30 s at medium intensity.

steps.title: Cut the tip off of a...
steps.instruction: Cut the tip off of a 1,000-µl pipette tip and transfer the gel mixture into a microcentrifuge tube filter.

steps.title: Centrifuge the mixture at 20,000g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the mixture at 20,000g for 3 min at room temperature.

steps.title: The NET-seq library is PCR amplified...
steps.instruction: The NET-seq library is PCR amplified using an Illumina index forward primer (Barcode primer, see Table 1) and a reverse primer (oNTI231; Table 1) specific to NET-seq (Figure 4). For each NET-seq library preparation, the minimum number of PCR cycles needed to obtain a sequencing library must to be determined. The NET-seq library is then amplified with the determined minimal amount of PCR cycles; this avoids overamplification which can result in unwanted products such as PCR duplicates.

steps.title: Prepare the PCR master mix (see section 2
steps.instruction: Prepare the PCR master mix (see section 2.4.8 step 4) for four pilot PCR amplifications for each cDNA sample and the oGAB11 cDNA control sample (denoted as O in figure). Mix well and store it on ice.

steps.title: For each PCR sample, put 19...
steps.instruction: For each PCR sample, put 19 µl of PCR master mix in a 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well.

steps.title: Perform PCR pilot amplifications (described in Table 2)
steps.instruction: Perform PCR pilot amplifications (described in Table 2). Remove one PCR tube for each sample at the end of the extension step after 6, 8, 10 and 12 amplification cycles.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.4 µl of 6× DNA loading dye to each tube and mix well.

steps.title: Prepare DNA control ladder (denoted as...
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA control ladder (denoted as L in figure) by adding 1.0 µl of ladder to 9 µl of DNase-free H2O. Then add 2 µl of 6× DNA loading dye and mix well.

steps.title: Separate the PCR products and the...
steps.instruction: Separate the PCR products and the DNA control ladder by TBE gel electrophoresis on an 8% (wt/vol) TBE gel at 180 V for 55 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution (see section 2.4.8) for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light...
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light and identify the optimal PCR amplification cycles for each cDNA sequencing library. The optimal PCR amplification cycles for the NET-seq library, which runs at ~150 nt, is determined by the presence of a clear band at ~150 nt and no higher-molecular-weight PCR products (the optimal number of cycles in the figure below is between 6 and 8).
3.1 Isolation of nuclei fraction

steps.title: Spray down all worksurfaces, pipettes, etc
steps.instruction: Spray down all worksurfaces, pipettes, etc. with RNase away.

steps.title: Prepare CLB, SB, NWB, GB, NLB,...
steps.instruction: Prepare CLB, SB, NWB, GB, NLB, and CRS buffers (see Materials section 1.1 for buffer components) and store all solutions on ice.

steps.title: Make sure tabletop centrifuge is at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Make sure tabletop centrifuge is at 4 °C.

steps.title: Cellular fractionation has been successfully performed...
steps.instruction: Cellular fractionation has been successfully performed on several different cell types (see Note 19 in the Guidelines section). The protocol was originally optimized for HeLa S3 or HEK293T cells (Mayer and Churchman 2016) . When fractionating other cell types optimization may be required (see Note 20). Cells are ideally grown according to internationally recognized guidelines (such as those suggested by ENCODE) and handled according to the supplier’s instructions (see Note 21 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: The following steps are for suspension...
steps.instruction: The following steps are for suspension cells, if working with adherent cells see Note 22 for steps on how to prepare the cells.

steps.title: Count out 1×107 cells as input...
steps.instruction: Count out 1×107 cells as input for each fractionation, including a western blot control sample (see Note 23). If cell numbers are limited, fractionation can be performed on fewer cells (see Note 24).

steps.title: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g...
steps.instruction: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g for 2 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Wash with 10 ml of PBS (room temperature)
steps.instruction: Wash with 10 ml of PBS (room temperature).

steps.title: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g...
steps.instruction: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g for 2 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add spike-in cells (Drosophila S2, used...
steps.instruction: Add spike-in cells (Drosophila S2, used for normalization) to the sample cells collected by centrifugation (the addition of a spike-in is optional, see Note 25).

steps.title: Repeat wash (step 3) and centrifugation (step 4)
steps.instruction: Repeat wash (step 3) and centrifugation (step 4).

steps.title: Remove supernatant by aspiration
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant by aspiration. It is important to remove the supernatant completely to avoid diluting the lysis buffer (see Note 26).

steps.title: Using a cut P1000 pipette tip,...
steps.instruction: Using a cut P1000 pipette tip, add 200 µl of CLB to cells and mix by pipetting the sample up and down ten times. Transfer sample to an RNase-free 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube using a cut 1,000 µl pipette tip.

steps.title: Incubate the cell lysate on ice for 5 min
steps.instruction: Incubate the cell lysate on ice for 5 min.

steps.title: During incubation period, pipette 500 µl...
steps.instruction: During incubation period, pipette 500 µl of SB to an RNase-free 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: After incubation period, use a cut...
steps.instruction: After incubation period, use a cut 1,000 µl pipette tip to layer the cell lysate sample onto the SB (see Note 27).

steps.title: Centrifuge layered lysate at 16,000g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge layered lysate at 16,000g for 10 min at 4 °C to collect nuclei.

steps.title: Pipette off supernatant completely from the...
steps.instruction: Pipette off supernatant completely from the nuclei pellet (supernatant contains the cytoplasmic fraction, retain for western blot, described in Box 1)

steps.title: Wash nuclei pellet with 800 µl...
steps.instruction: Wash nuclei pellet with 800 µl of NWB (either by inverting the tube or pipetting up and down).

steps.title: Collect cell nuclei by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Collect cell nuclei by centrifugation at 1,150g for 1 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Pipette off supernatant from the nuclei pellet
steps.instruction: Pipette off supernatant from the nuclei pellet. It is important to remove the supernatant completely in order to remove cytoplasmic mature RNAs (see Note 26).

steps.title: Add 200 µl of GB to the nuclei
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl of GB to the nuclei. Use a cut 1,000 µl tip to resuspend the washed nuclei pellet by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Transfer the nuclei suspension to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the nuclei suspension to a new 1.5 ml RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Layer 200 µl of NLB onto...
steps.instruction: Layer 200 µl of NLB onto nuclei suspension and mix by pulsed vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate the mixture on ice for 2 min
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture on ice for 2 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 18,500g for 2 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 18,500g for 2 min at 4 °C to collect the chromatin pellet.

steps.title: Pipette off supernatant from the chromatin pellet
steps.instruction: Pipette off supernatant from the chromatin pellet. It is important to completely remove the supernatant in order to remove nucleoplasmic RNAs (retain supernatant for the nucleoplasm fraction of western blot, described in Box 1).

steps.title: Add 50 µl CRS to the...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl CRS to the chromatin pellet, transfer pellet in CRS to a new 1.5 ml tube and then resuspend pellet in CRS (see Note 28). Perform this step on the experimental sample (for the preparation of nascent RNA, section 3.3.3) and the control sample (for the western blot analysis, as described in Box 1).

steps.title: Add 700 µl of QIAzol (lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µl of QIAzol (lysis reagent in miRNeasy mini kit, Qiagen) to the resuspended chromatin (see Note 10 re QIAzol).

steps.title: Solubilize chromatin pellet by slowly pipetting...
steps.instruction: Solubilize chromatin pellet by slowly pipetting up and down using a 1-ml syringe with a 22G needle (see Note 29). Mix very carefully (to avoid spilling the sample) until the solution is homogeneous.

steps.title: Prepare RNA using the miRNeasy mini...
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA using the miRNeasy mini kit (or miRNeasy micro kit for small cell numbers, see section 2.3.3 step 2) according to the manufacturer’s instructions, including optional DNase treatment.

steps.title: Measure the prepared RNA quantity and...
steps.instruction: Measure the prepared RNA quantity and quality using a NanoDrop (see Note 30). The RNA yield is usually in the range of 20–30 µg (this can vary quite a lot depending on the type of cells used). If fractionation was performed on less than 1×107 cells (or RNA yield is less than 20 µg) see Note 24 re RNA amount needed to construct sequencing library. Store the isolated RNA at −80 °C (can be stored for months) until ready to construct sequencing library.

steps.title: Spray down all work surfaces, pipettes, etc
steps.instruction: Spray down all work surfaces, pipettes, etc. with RNase away

steps.title: Denature the RNA sample (from the cellular fractionation, section 3
steps.instruction: Denature the RNA sample (from the cellular fractionation, section 3.3.3) and 5 µl of the oGAB11 control (10 µM; Table 1) for 2 min at 80 °C in a Thermomixer. Place on ice.

steps.title: Prepare linker ligation mix for each...
steps.instruction: Prepare linker ligation mix for each RNA sample and for oGAB11 in 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tubes. Mix until reaction is homogeneous, poor mixing will negatively affect the ligation efficiency.

steps.title: Add 1 µl Truncated T4 RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl Truncated T4 RNA ligase 2 (200U) to each RNA sample as well as the oGAB11 control.

steps.title: Incubate the ligation mixes for 10...
steps.instruction: Incubate the ligation mixes for 10 - 16 hours at 16 °C in a thermal cycler (Note 31).

steps.title: Place on ice and add 0
steps.instruction: Place on ice and add 0.7 µl of EDTA (0.5 M) to each ligation mix to stop the ligation reaction.

steps.title: Perform fragmentation calibration test (see Box 2)
steps.instruction: Perform fragmentation calibration test (see Box 2)

steps.title: Add 20 µl of 2× alkaline...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µl of 2× alkaline fragmentation solution to each sample (from the ligation reaction) and mix. Do not perform fragmentation steps for the oGAB11 ligation control.

steps.title: Incubate the sample at 95 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample at 95 °C in a thermal cycler for the calibrated time (see Box 2) to fragment RNA (see Note 32).

steps.title: To purify the fragmented RNA, use...
steps.instruction: To purify the fragmented RNA, use the RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit (Zymo) and elute RNA from column with 10 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.0). Alternatively, RNA can be prepared by RNA precipitation (see Note 33).

steps.title: Prepare RNA ladder and un-ligated oGAB11 control by adding 1
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA ladder and un-ligated oGAB11 control by adding 1.0 µl of RNA control ladder or oGAB11 to 9 µl of RNase-free H2O. Add 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample to the RNA ladder and oGAB11 control, mix well (see Note 34).

steps.title: Prepare all RNA samples (including the...
steps.instruction: Prepare all RNA samples (including the ligated oGAB11 control) by adding 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer to each sample, mix well.

steps.title: Denature the RNA sample, RNA control...
steps.instruction: Denature the RNA sample, RNA control samples (ligated oGAB11 and un-ligated oGAB11 controls), and RNA ladder for 2 min at 80 °C. Place the samples on ice.

steps.title: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea...
steps.instruction: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel at 200 V for 15 min in 1× TBE. Thoroughly flush out gel wells prior to pre-running the gel (see Note 35).

steps.title: Load gel with the RNA ladder,...
steps.instruction: Load gel with the RNA ladder, RNA control samples (ligated oGAB11 and un-ligated oGAB11), and the fragmented RNA sample. Separate by PAGE at 200 V for 65 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light.

steps.title: For the fragmented RNA sample, excise...
steps.instruction: For the fragmented RNA sample, excise region between 40 and 110 nt. For the ligated oGAB11, excise a narrow band ~55 nt (see Note 36).

steps.title: Perform rapid gel extraction on the...
steps.instruction: Perform rapid gel extraction on the excised gel (see Box 3).

steps.title: After rapid gel extraction is performed,...
steps.instruction: After rapid gel extraction is performed, use the RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit (Zymo) and elute RNA from column with 10 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.0) (at this point, RNA can be stored up to 3 months at -80 °C). Alternatively, RNA can be prepared by RNA precipitation (see Note 33).

steps.title: The following steps are for suspension...
steps.instruction: The following steps are for suspension cells, if working with adherent cells see Note 22 in the Guidelines section for steps on how to prepare the cells.

steps.title: Count out 1×10 7 cells as...
steps.instruction: Count out 1×10 7 cells as input for each fractionation, including a western blot control sample (see Note 23 in the Guidelines section). If cell numbers are limited, fractionation can be performed on fewer cells (see Note 24 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g...
steps.instruction: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g for 2 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Wash with 10 ml of PBS (room temperature)
steps.instruction: Wash with 10 ml of PBS (room temperature).

steps.title: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g...
steps.instruction: Collect cells by centrifugation at 500g for 2 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add spike-in cells (Drosophila S2, used...
steps.instruction: Add spike-in cells (Drosophila S2, used for normalization) to the sample cells collected by centrifugation (the addition of a spike-in is optional, see Note 25 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Repeat wash (step 8) and centrifugation (step 9)
steps.instruction: Repeat wash (step 8) and centrifugation (step 9).

steps.title: Remove supernatant by aspiration
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant by aspiration. It is important to remove the supernatant completely to avoid diluting the lysis buffer (see Note 26 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Using a cut P1000 pipette tip,...
steps.instruction: Using a cut P1000 pipette tip, add 200 µl of CLB to cells and mix by pipetting the sample up and down ten times. Transfer sample to an RNase-free 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube using a cut 1,000 µl pipette tip.

steps.title: Incubate the cell lysate on ice for 5 min
steps.instruction: Incubate the cell lysate on ice for 5 min.

steps.title: During incubation period, pipette 500 µl...
steps.instruction: During incubation period, pipette 500 µl of SB to an RNase-free 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: After incubation period, use a cut...
steps.instruction: After incubation period, use a cut 1,000 µl pipette tip to layer the cell lysate sample onto the SB (see Note 27 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Centrifuge layered lysate at 16,000g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge layered lysate at 16,000g for 10 min at 4 °C to collect nuclei.

steps.title: Pipette off supernatant completely from the...
steps.instruction: Pipette off supernatant completely from the nuclei pellet (supernatant contains the cytoplasmic fraction, retain for western blot, described in Box 1)

steps.title: Wash nuclei pellet with 800 µl...
steps.instruction: Wash nuclei pellet with 800 µl of NWB (either by inverting the tube or pipetting up and down using a cut pipette tip).

steps.title: Collect cell nuclei by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Collect cell nuclei by centrifugation at 1,150g for 1 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Pipette off supernatant from the nuclei pellet
steps.instruction: Pipette off supernatant from the nuclei pellet. It is important to remove the supernatant completely in order to remove cytoplasmic mature RNAs (see Note 26 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Add 200 µl of GB to the nuclei
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl of GB to the nuclei. Use a cut 1,000 µl tip to resuspend the washed nuclei pellet by pipetting up and down.

steps.title: Transfer the nuclei suspension to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the nuclei suspension to a new 1.5 ml RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Layer 200 µl of NLB onto...
steps.instruction: Layer 200 µl of NLB onto nuclei suspension and briefly mix by pulsed vortexing.

steps.title: Incubate the mixture on ice for 2 min
steps.instruction: Incubate the mixture on ice for 2 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 18,500g for 2 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 18,500g for 2 min at 4 °C to collect the chromatin pellet.

steps.title: Pipette off supernatant from the chromatin pellet
steps.instruction: Pipette off supernatant from the chromatin pellet. It is important to completely remove the supernatant in order to remove nucleoplasmic RNAs (retain supernatant for the nucleoplasm fraction of western blot, described in Box 1).

steps.title: Add 50 µl CRS to the...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl CRS to the chromatin pellet, transfer pellet in CRS to a new 1.5 ml tube and then resuspend pellet in CRS (see Note 28 in the Guidelines section). Perform this step on the experimental sample (for the preparation of nascent RNA, section 1.3.3) and the control sample (for the western blot analysis, as described in Box 1).

steps.title: Add 700 µl of QIAzol (lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 700 µl of QIAzol (lysis reagent in miRNeasy mini kit, Qiagen) to the resuspended chromatin (see Note 10 in the Guidelines section re QIAzol).

steps.title: Solubilize chromatin pellet by slowly pipetting...
steps.instruction: Solubilize chromatin pellet by slowly pipetting up and down using a 1-ml syringe with a 22G needle (see Note 29 in the Guidelines section). Mix very carefully (to avoid spilling the sample) until the solution is homogeneous (at this point the sample can be stored at −80 °C for months).

steps.title: Prepare RNA using the miRNeasy mini...
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA using the miRNeasy mini kit (or miRNeasy micro kit for small cell numbers, see Materials section 1.3.3, Preparation of nascent RNA, step 2) according to the manufacturer’s instructions, including optional DNase treatment.

steps.title: Measure the prepared RNA quantity and...
steps.instruction: Measure the prepared RNA quantity and quality using a NanoDrop (see Note 30 in the Guidelines section). The RNA yield is usually in the range of 20–30 µg (this can vary quite a lot depending on the type of cells used). If fractionation was performed on less than 1×10 7 cells (or RNA yield is less than 20 µg) see Note 24 in the Guidelines section re RNA amount needed to construct sequencing library. Store the isolated RNA at −80 °C (can be stored for months) until ready to construct sequencing library.

steps.title: Prepare linker ligation mix for each...
steps.instruction: Prepare linker ligation mix for each RNA sample and for oGAB11 in 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tubes (see Materials section 1.4.1 , Linker ligation, step 4). Mix until reaction is homogeneous, poor mixing will negatively affect the ligation efficiency.

steps.title: Add 1 µl Truncated T4 RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl Truncated T4 RNA ligase 2 (200U) to each RNA sample as well as the oGAB11 control.

steps.title: Incubate the ligation samples overnight for...
steps.instruction: Incubate the ligation samples overnight for 10 - 16 hours at 16 °C in a thermal cycler (Note 31 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Place on ice and add 0
steps.instruction: Place on ice and add 0.7 µl of EDTA (0.5 M) to each ligation sample to stop the ligation reaction.

steps.title: Perform fragmentation calibration test (see Box 2)
steps.instruction: Perform fragmentation calibration test (see Box 2)

steps.title: Add 20 µl of 2× alkaline...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µl of 2× alkaline fragmentation solution (see Materials section 1.4.2 , RNA fragmentation, step 1) to each sample (from the ligation reaction) and mix. Do not perform fragmentation steps for the oGAB11 ligation control.

steps.title: Incubate the sample at 95 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample at 95 °C in a thermal cycler for the calibrated time (see Box 2) to fragment RNA (see Note 32 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: To purify the fragmented RNA, use...
steps.instruction: To purify the fragmented RNA, use the RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit (Zymo) and elute RNA from column with 10 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.0). Alternatively, RNA can be prepared by RNA precipitation (see Note 33 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Prepare RNA ladder and un-ligated oGAB11 control by adding 1
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA ladder and un-ligated oGAB11 control by adding 1.0 µl of RNA control ladder or oGAB11 to 9 µl of RNase-free H2O. Add 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample to the RNA ladder and oGAB11 control, mix well (see Note 34 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Prepare all RNA samples (including the...
steps.instruction: Prepare all RNA samples (including the ligated oGAB11 control) by adding 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer to each sample, mix well.

steps.title: Denature the RNA sample, RNA control...
steps.instruction: Denature the RNA sample, RNA control samples (ligated oGAB11 and un-ligated oGAB11 controls), and RNA ladder for 2 min at 80 °C. Place the samples on ice.

steps.title: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea...
steps.instruction: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel at 200 V for 15 min in 1× TBE. Thoroughly flush out gel wells prior to pre-running the gel (see Note 35 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Load gel with the RNA ladder,...
steps.instruction: Load gel with the RNA ladder, RNA control samples (ligated oGAB11 and un-ligated oGAB11), and the fragmented RNA sample. Separate by PAGE at 200 V for 65 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light.

steps.title: For the fragmented RNA sample, excise...
steps.instruction: For the fragmented RNA sample, excise region between 40 and 110 nt. For the ligated oGAB11, excise a narrow band ~55 nt (see Note 36 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Perform rapid gel extraction on the...
steps.instruction: Perform rapid gel extraction on the excised gel (see Box 3).

steps.title: After rapid gel extraction is performed,...
steps.instruction: After rapid gel extraction is performed, use the RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit (Zymo) and elute RNA from column with 10 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.0) (at this point, RNA can be stored up to 3 months at -80 °C). Alternatively, RNA can be prepared by RNA precipitation (see Note 33 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Add 4
steps.instruction: Add 4.6 µl of RT reaction mix to 10 µl of the RNA sample and the oGAB11 control sample.

steps.title: Denature the sample mixtures (including the...
steps.instruction: Denature the sample mixtures (including the oGAB11 control) by incubating for 2 min at 80 °C in a thermal cycler. Place on ice.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.3 µl of SUPERase.In/DTT mix (see Materials section 1.4.4 , Reverse Transcription, step 4) and mix well.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.8 µl of SuperScript III (200 U/µl) and mix.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min at 48...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min at 48 °C in a thermal cycler.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.8 µl of 1 N NaOH, mix well and incubate 20 min at 98 °C. This step degrades the RNA after cDNA was produced by RT.

steps.title: Neutralize the reaction by adding 1
steps.instruction: Neutralize the reaction by adding 1.8 µl of 1 N HCl; mix well and place on ice.

steps.title: Prepare the DNA control ladder
steps.instruction: Prepare the DNA control ladder. Add 1.0 µl of DNA control ladder to 9 µl of RNase-free H2O. Add 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer and mix.

steps.title: Add 20 µl of 2× TBU...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer to the cDNA sample and the oGAB11 cDNA control, mix well.

steps.title: Denature the cDNA sample, oGAB11 cDNA...
steps.instruction: Denature the cDNA sample, oGAB11 cDNA control and DNA control ladder for 3 min at 95 °C in a Thermomixer. Place the samples on ice.

steps.title: Prerun a 10% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea...
steps.instruction: Prerun a 10% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel at 200 V for 15 min in 1× TBE. Thoroughly flush out gel wells prior to pre-running the gel (see Note 35 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Load gel (20 uL per lane,...
steps.instruction: Load gel (20 uL per lane, each sample will be split into two lanes) with the cDNA sample, the oGAB11 cDNA control and the DNA ladder. Separate by PAGE at 200 V for 65 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light.

steps.title: For the cDNA sample, excise region...
steps.instruction: For the cDNA sample, excise region between ~85 and 160 nt (use oGAB11 as baseline and cut up ~70 nt). For the cDNA oGAB11 control, excise a narrow band ~85 nt. If no cDNA smear is observed see Note 37 in the Guidelines section.

steps.title: Perform rapid gel extraction on the...
steps.instruction: Perform rapid gel extraction on the excised gel (see Box 3). There will be two gels for one sample since each sample was split into two lanes, combine the two gels from one sample into a single tube.

steps.title: After rapid gel extraction is performed,...
steps.instruction: After rapid gel extraction is performed, use the Oligo kit (Zymo) and elute cDNA from column with 15 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) (at this point, the cDNA can be stored indefinitely at −20 °C). Alternatively, cDNA can be prepared by precipitation (see Note 33 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: In a 0
steps.instruction: In a 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tube, add 4 µl of circularization mix to 15 µl of the cDNA sample and the oGAB11 cDNA control sample, mix well.

steps.title: Add 1 µl of CircLigase (100 U/µl) and mix
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of CircLigase (100 U/µl) and mix.

steps.title: Incubate the CircLigase reaction for 60...
steps.instruction: Incubate the CircLigase reaction for 60 min at 60 °C and then 10 min at 80 °C in a thermal cycler. Circularized cDNA can be stored indefinitely at −20 °C.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: at this point a specific depletion of highly abundant mature RNAs can be performed (see Note 38 in the Guidelines section)

steps.title: Perform PCR amplification test (Box 4)
steps.instruction: Perform PCR amplification test (Box 4)

steps.title: Prepare PCR mix for four PCR...
steps.instruction: Prepare PCR mix for four PCR amplification reactions for both the cDNA sample and the oGAB11 control cDNA sample (see Materials section 1.4.8 , PCR amplification, step 4). Mix well and store on ice.

steps.title: For each PCR, put 19 µl...
steps.instruction: For each PCR, put 19 µl of PCR master mix in a 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well.

steps.title: Perform PCR amplifications (as shown in...
steps.instruction: Perform PCR amplifications (as shown in Table 2) with determined optimal number of PCR cycles (see Box 4). TABLE 2 | PCR amplification %s %s Cycle numberDenatureAnnealExtend198 °C, 30 s 2–1498 °C, 10 s60 °C, 10 s72 °C, 5 s

steps.title: Prepare DNA control ladder
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA control ladder. Add 1.0 µl of DNA control ladder to 9 µl of DNase-free H2O. Add 2 µl of 6× DNA loading dye and mix well.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.4 µl of 6× DNA loading dye to each PCR sample tube and mix well.

steps.title: Load gel with the PCR products and DNA control ladder
steps.instruction: Load gel with the PCR products and DNA control ladder. Separate by TBE gel electrophoresis on an 8% (wt/vol) TBE gel at 180 V for 55 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light.

steps.title: For each sample, excise the band...
steps.instruction: For each sample, excise the band from the gel that contains the PCR product. The NET-seq library is a broad band that runs at ~150 nt (use oGAB as baseline and cut up ~50 nt). Avoid contamination from the lower band that runs at ~120 nt, representing PCR product from empty circles (see Note 39). The oGAB11 control sample is not subjected to this step, or any further steps.

steps.title: Perform the first four steps of...
steps.instruction: Perform the first four steps of rapid gel extraction (see Box 3)

steps.title: After centrifugation, add 670 µl of...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, add 670 µl of DNA soaking buffer and mix.

steps.title: Incubate the samples overnight at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples overnight at room temperature in a Thermomixer (set at 1,500 r.p.m.).

steps.title: Use a cut 1,000 µl pipette...
steps.instruction: Use a cut 1,000 µl pipette tip to transfer the gel slurry into a microcentrifuge tube filter.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 3 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 3 min at room temperature. Transfer the eluate to a new 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Precipitate the NET-seq library by adding...
steps.instruction: Precipitate the NET-seq library by adding 2 µl of 15 mg/ml GlycoBlue and 680 µl of isopropanol. Mix well then incubate the precipitations at −20 °C for ≥1 h (the DNA precipitations can be stored at −20 °C overnight).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 30 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 20,000g for 30 min at 4 °C to pellet the RNA.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and wash the pellet with 750 µl of 80% (vol/vol) ice-cold ethanol.

steps.title: Spin the sample at 20,000g for...
steps.instruction: Spin the sample at 20,000g for 2 min at 4 °C, and then remove the supernatant. Air-dry the RNA pellet for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Resuspend the NET-seq library in 7...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the NET-seq library in 7 - 10 µl of Tris-HCl (10 mM, pH 8.0). The DNA sequencing library can be stored indefinitely at −20 °C.

steps.title: Prepare a 1:5 dilution of the...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1:5 dilution of the NET-seq library by adding 1 µl of the NET-seq library to 4 µl of Tris-HCl (10 mM, pH 8.0); mix well (see Note 40 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Use 1 µl of the diluted...
steps.instruction: Use 1 µl of the diluted NET-seq library for quantification with the Qubit fluorometer using the Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit. Prepare the sample and perform the measurement according to the manufacturer’s protocol (see Note 41 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Use 1 µl of the diluted...
steps.instruction: Use 1 µl of the diluted NET-seq library for characterization on the Agilent Bioanalyzer; use the high-sensitivity DNA analysis kit according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Sequence using oLSC006
steps.instruction: Sequence using oLSC006. Sequence the human NET-seq library from the 3′ end (75 bp single-end sequencing) on the Illumina platform using oLSC006 (Table 1 in the Materials section) as a custom sequencing primer. Perform sequencing according to the manufacturer’s instructions. NET-seq libraries are typically sequenced on MiSeq, HiSeq or NextSeq next-generation sequencing platforms. A reasonable coverage is obtained with 100–200 million reads.

steps.title: Assess read quality control with FastQC
steps.instruction: Assess read quality control with FastQC.

steps.title: Displace 5’ end random barcode sequence...
steps.instruction: Displace 5’ end random barcode sequence from sequence to read identifier line and save as a separate fastq file. This barcode serves as a unique molecular identifier (UMI) and originates from the linker (see Table 1 in the Materials section).

steps.title: Align fastq files with and without...
steps.instruction: Align fastq files with and without UMI sequences separately to a human reference genome using STAR (See Note 43 in the Guidelines section). In case a Drosophila spike-in was used, concatenate the human and Drosophila fasta files to generate a combined reference genome.

steps.title: Remove alignments resulting from RT mispriming events, i
steps.instruction: Remove alignments resulting from RT mispriming events, i.e. cases where RT priming did not occur at the complementary sequence site on the ligated linker but instead at a position within the RNA fragment. The resulting reads can be identified as they lack the random UMI sequence encoded on the linker and therefore align well to the reference genome both with and without UMI displacement: when the STAR alignment score of a read with the UMI sequence equals the UMI length (10nt) plus the alignment score from that read without UMI.

steps.title: Remove alignments that map to multiple...
steps.instruction: Remove alignments that map to multiple genomic locations by filtering for their MAPQ value (< 50) using samtools.

steps.title: Remove alignments resulting from PCR duplication events, i
steps.instruction: Remove alignments resulting from PCR duplication events, i.e. cases of multiple amplicons that originate from the same RNA fragment. The resulting multiple reads are bioinformatically indistinguishable as their alignments share the same position, strand, chromosome, UMI sequence and CIGAR score. To filter out these PCR duplicates, we maintain only one of the alignments.

steps.title: Remove alignments that could arise from splicing intermediates, i
steps.instruction: Remove alignments that could arise from splicing intermediates, i.e. 3’ ends of annotated exons and introns.

steps.title: Use HTseq to generate a coverage...
steps.instruction: Use HTseq to generate a coverage (bedgraph) file containing the read counts at a base-pair resolution over the reference genome as determined from the 5’ end of the read alignments (without UMI) on the opposite strand. The resulting positions correspond to the 3’ ends of nascent RNA and thereby map RNA polymerase positions. The coverage files will be used for a variety of downstream bioinformatics analyses such as visual inspection (e.g. using IGV), metagene profiling (e.g. Bedtools), differential expression (e.g. DEseq2).

steps.title: The western blot control is used...
steps.instruction: The western blot control is used to determine whether or not the cellular fractionation protocol has been executed successfully. The efficiency of the fractionation can be quantified by measuring the amount of elongating Pol II captured in the chromatin fraction (ideally ≥95%). When the fractionation protocol is being performed for the first time or being tested on a new cell type, the cytoplasm, nucleoplasm, and chromatin fractions should be checked for subcellular marker proteins (see Note 5 in the Guidelines section) to confirm the fractionation is working (see Note 20 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: and chromatin fraction (from steps 1
steps.instruction: and chromatin fraction (from steps 1.3.

steps.title: volumes to the cytoplasmic fraction (from steps 1
steps.instruction: volumes to the cytoplasmic fraction (from steps 1.3.

steps.title: volume by adding 1× PBS
steps.instruction: volume by adding 1× PBS. Mix each fraction well and ensure the chromatin pellet is completely resuspended (see Note 7 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Add 50 µl of 2× SDS...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl of 2× SDS buffer (see Materials section 1.3.2, Isolation of chromatin fraction, step 7) to 50 µl of each subcellular fraction and boil the samples at 95 °C for 5 min (at this point, samples can be stored for months at −20 °C).

steps.title: Separate samples by standard SDS-PAGE (loading...
steps.instruction: Separate samples by standard SDS-PAGE (loading 10 µl of each boiled sample per lane is usually sufficient).

steps.title: Probe the membrane overnight at 4...
steps.instruction: Probe the membrane overnight at 4 °C with Pol II CTD Ser2-P antibody (3E10, 1:1,000 dilution) and Pol II CTD Ser5-P antibody (3E8, 1:1,000 dilution). These antibodies target transcribing RNA Pol II (see Note 6 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Take 9 samples of RNA (~1ug...
steps.instruction: Take 9 samples of RNA (~1ug each) from a total RNA sample (or any other easily obtained RNA sample). Re-suspend each RNA sample in 8uL of RNase-free water and add 12uL of the linker ligation mix, excluding the linker and ligase (see Materials section 1.4.1, Linker ligation, step 4). Add 20 µl of 2× alkaline fragmentation solution (see Materials section 1.4.2 , RNA fragmentation, step 1) to each sample and mix well (see Note 32 in the Guidelines section); fragmentation is not performed for the oGAB11 control.

steps.title: Fragment each RNA sample at 95...
steps.instruction: Fragment each RNA sample at 95 °C in a thermal cycler for the following times: 0, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, and 50 minutes.

steps.title: To purify the fragmented RNA, use...
steps.instruction: To purify the fragmented RNA, use the RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit (Zymo) and elute RNA from column with 10 uL of 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.0). Alternatively, RNA can be prepared by RNA precipitation (see Note 33 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Prepare RNA ladder (denoted as L...
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA ladder (denoted as L in figure) by adding 1.0 µl of RNA control ladder to 9 µl of RNase-free H2O. Add 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample to the RNA ladder and mix well.

steps.title: Prepare all RNA samples by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare all RNA samples by adding 10 µl of 2× TBU denaturing sample buffer to each sample, mix well.

steps.title: Denature the RNA sample and RNA...
steps.instruction: Denature the RNA sample and RNA ladder for 2 min at 80 °C. Place the samples on ice.

steps.title: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea...
steps.instruction: Pre-run a 15% (wt/vol) polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel at 200 V for 15 min in 1× TBE. Thoroughly flush out gel wells prior to pre-running the gel (see Note 35 in the Guidelines section).

steps.title: Load gel with the RNA ladder...
steps.instruction: Load gel with the RNA ladder and the fragmented RNA sample. Separate by PAGE at 200 V for 65 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light. The optimal fragmentation time is when most RNA molecules are in the required size range, between 40 and 110 nt (the optimal fragmentation time in the figure below is between 25 and 30 minutes).

steps.title: Use a 21G needle to pierce...
steps.instruction: Use a 21G needle to pierce the bottom of a 0.5-ml RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Place the pierced 0
steps.instruction: Place the pierced 0.5-ml tube into a 1.5-ml RNase-free microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Put the gel slice (from fragmentation,...
steps.instruction: Put the gel slice (from fragmentation, RT, or PCR) into the pierced 0.5-ml tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge the nestled tubes, containing the...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the nestled tubes, containing the gel slice, at 20,000g for 4 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 200 µl of RNase-free H2O...
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl of RNase-free H2O to the chopped-up gel and mix.

steps.title: Incubate the sample mixture for 10...
steps.instruction: Incubate the sample mixture for 10 min at 70 °C in a Thermomixer.

steps.title: Vortex the mixture for 30 s at medium intensity
steps.instruction: Vortex the mixture for 30 s at medium intensity.

steps.title: Cut the tip off of a...
steps.instruction: Cut the tip off of a 1,000-µl pipette tip and transfer the gel mixture into a microcentrifuge tube filter.

steps.title: Centrifuge the mixture at 20,000g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the mixture at 20,000g for 3 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Prepare the PCR master mix (see Materials section 1
steps.instruction: Prepare the PCR master mix (see Materials section 1.4.8, PCR amplification, step 4) for four pilot PCR amplifications for each cDNA sample and the oGAB11 cDNA control sample (denoted as O in figure). Mix well and store it on ice.

steps.title: For each PCR sample, put 19...
steps.instruction: For each PCR sample, put 19 µl of PCR master mix in a 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tube.

steps.title: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well
steps.instruction: Add 1 µl of circularized cDNA and mix it well.

steps.title: Perform PCR pilot amplifications (described in Table 2)
steps.instruction: Perform PCR pilot amplifications (described in Table 2). Remove one PCR tube for each sample at the end of the extension step after 6, 8, 10 and 12 amplification cycles.

steps.title: Add 3
steps.instruction: Add 3.4 µl of 6× DNA loading dye to each tube and mix well.

steps.title: Prepare DNA control ladder (denoted as...
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA control ladder (denoted as L in figure) by adding 1.0 µl of ladder to 9 µl of DNase-free H2O. Then add 2 µl of 6× DNA loading dye and mix well.

steps.title: Separate the PCR products and the...
steps.instruction: Separate the PCR products and the DNA control ladder by TBE gel electrophoresis on an 8% (wt/vol) TBE gel at 180 V for 55 min.

steps.title: Stain the gel in 50 ml...
steps.instruction: Stain the gel in 50 ml of gel staining solution (see Materials section 1.4.8, PCR amplification) for 5 min at room temperature on a shaker (use a black gel box to protect gel from light while staining).

steps.title: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light...
steps.instruction: Visualize the gel under blue/UV light and identify the optimal PCR amplification cycles for each cDNA sequencing library. The optimal PCR amplification cycles for the NET-seq library, which runs at ~150 nt, is determined by the presence of a clear band at ~150 nt and no higher-molecular-weight PCR products (the optimal number of cycles in the figure above is between 6 and 8).

steps.title: Prepare RT reaction mix in a 0
steps.instruction: Prepare RT reaction mix in a 0.2-ml RNase-free PCR tube (see Materials section 1.4.4 , Reverse Transcription, step 3), store on ice.

steps.title: Denature the RNA sample (from the cellular fractionation, section 1
steps.instruction: Denature the RNA sample (from the cellular fractionation, section 1.3.3) and 5 µl of the oGAB11 control (10 µM; Table 1 in the Materials section) for 2 min at 80 °C in a Thermomixer. Place on ice.

steps.title: Prepare circularization mix (see Materials section 1
steps.instruction: Prepare circularization mix (see Materials section 1.4.7 , Circularization of cDNA, step 2) and store it on ice.

steps.title: Below we describe in brief the...
steps.instruction: Below we describe in brief the custom bioinformatics pipeline (see Figure 5) that is used to generate NET-seq coverage files. The scripts are available at Churchman lab GitHub . See Note 42 in the Guidelines section for a description of the improvements relative to our previous pipeline (Mayer et al. 2015).

steps.title: Performing RNA fragmentation results in a...
steps.instruction: Performing RNA fragmentation results in a homogeneous RNA pool which helps to avoid length biases in any downstream enzymatic reactions. The fragmentation test can be performed using total RNA (or any easily-obtained RNA sample). Once the appropriate fragmentation time is determined (where most of the RNA molecules range from 40 –110 nt), fragmentation can be performed on the nascent RNA (obtained from cellular fractionation) using the determined time (see directly above); the size range of the nascent RNA and total RNA pools do not vary enough for the fragmentation time to be different.

steps.title: This protocol for rapidly extracting RNA...
steps.instruction: This protocol for rapidly extracting RNA or cDNA from a polyacrylamide TBE-urea gel is similar to the gel extraction protocol described by the Weissman laboratory (Ingolia et al. 2012; Churchman and Weissman 2012).

steps.title: The NET-seq library is PCR amplified...
steps.instruction: The NET-seq library is PCR amplified using an Illumina index forward primer (Barcode primer, see Table 1 in the Materials section) and a reverse primer (oNTI231; Table 1 in the Materials section) specific to NET-seq (Figure 4). For each NET-seq library preparation, the minimum number of PCR cycles needed to obtain a sequencing library must to be determined. The NET-seq library is then amplified with the determined minimal amount of PCR cycles; this avoids overamplification which can result in unwanted products such as PCR duplicates.

steps.title: Spray down all work surfaces, pipettes, etc
steps.instruction: Spray down all work surfaces, pipettes, etc. with RNase away

steps.title: Figure 2| RNA ligation, fragmentation and size selection
steps.instruction: Figure 2| RNA ligation, fragmentation and size selection. RNA is ligated to the Linker overnight and then fragmented for an optimal period of time (Box 2). The fragmented RNA (lanes A and B) is separated on a 15% (wt/vol) TBE-urea gel and excised from the gel between 35 to 110 nt (excised area represented by the red dotted box). Lane C, DNA ladder; lane D, ligated oGAB11 control; lane E, un-ligated oGAB11. The RNA was obtained from K562 cells and ~1 µg of RNA was used per fragmentation reaction.

steps.title: Figure 3| Reverse transcription of RNA...
steps.instruction: Figure 3| Reverse transcription of RNA to cDNA, RNA degradation, and size selection of the cDNA.The RT product (lanes B and C) is separated on two lanes of a 10% (wt/vol) TBE-urea gel. The red dotted box shows the region where the cDNA is excised from the gel (in the range of 100–160 nt). Lane A, oGAB11 control; lane D, DNA ladder.

steps.title: Figure 4| Circularization followed by PCR amplification of cDNA
steps.instruction: Figure 4| Circularization followed by PCR amplification of cDNA. Size selection and quantification (bioanalyzer trace) of the PCR product. The PCR product (lanes A, B, and C) is separated on an 8% (wt/vol) TBE gel and excised from the gel between 150 to 200 nt (excised area represented by the red dotted box). The PCR product that arises from circularized RT primer and the excess barcode primer are labeled on the gel image. Lane D, oGAB11 control; lane E, DNA ladder. The Bioanalyzer trace shows a human NET-seq library generated from 500 ng of RNA. The trace shows a major peak around 150 nt and a smaller peak around 120 nt (corresponding to the circularized RT primer product). The sequencing reads that result from this smaller peak are computationally removed. The peaks at 35 and 10380 nt are the manufacturer’s internal markers. FU, fluorescence units.

description: Figure 1 | (b) Schematic overview of the DNA sequencing library preparation (1.4). Figure 1| (a) Schematic overview of the cellular fractionation protocol (1.3). CLB, cytoplasmic lysis buffer; SB, sucrose buffer; NWB, nuclei wash buffer; GB, glycerol buffer; NLB, nuclei lysis buffer; CRS, cytoplasmic resuspension solution. Figure 5| Schematic overview of the bioinformatics pipeline and DNA strand–specific RNA polymerase II coverage at a representative gene, GAPDH (Glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate dehydrogenase). The Pol II density for HeLa S3 cells (Mayer et al. 2015) at the positive (+) and negative (−) DNA strand is shown in blue and red, respectively. The transcription start site as well as the direction of transcription are marked by a black arrow; and exonic regions are portrayed as blue boxes while intronic regions are portrayed as blue lines. Total number of raw reads in library: 169953553. Number of alignments after filtering steps: 37398108 (22% of total library).
